value:
v1.13.0 SHIPPED 2026-04-23 (tag 9e8f98c). Agent-first release from the 584-call, 15-day usage dataset.

SHIPPED IN v1.13.0 (11 issues):
- #91 cross-session handoff protocol (dcd2712)
- #92 MCP tool description audit (530d284)
- #93 audit log data quality gaps (0e8f813, 745c5a3)
- #94 codex_copy aliasResolved semantic (742eb41)
- #88 askPassword non-interactive — CCLI_PASSWORD + --password-file (19b8e0a)
- #39 interpolation syntax audit (127f4bd)
- #82 ccli lint --seed-quality (952aeae)
- #83 ccli topology (8120979)
- #86 softprops/action-gh-release v2→v3 (2b8d1cf)
- #89 shape-invalid import bypass (171b0e7)
- #90 hardcoded 'ccli' hint (a3aeb85)

Test count: 1269 → 1325 (+56). Three new namespaces/tools worth remembering for next session: --seed-quality heuristic checks, ccli topology co-occurrence analysis, context.next_session banner in codex_context.

DOGFOODING POST (Part Two): docs/dogfooding-real-usage.md + addendum (22a2d0f) covering the closure session — publishable alongside the v1.13.0 tag. The addendum reflects on SEQUENCING as the non-obvious lesson: #92 first (lowest-risk highest-leverage), #93 plumbing so the next cycle's signal is clean, then #91 design pass with the other two already in place.

NEXT CYCLE CANDIDATES (v1.14+ backlog, all unmilestoned):
- #84 unified entry health (lifecycle/coldness/staleness) — larger feature worth its own design pass
- #45 entry attribution (who/what last modified)
- #50 npx codexcli zero-install
- #47 ccli diff — compare local vs committed
- #6 Fish/PowerShell shell completion

NEXT DOGFOODING DATASET: wait for v1.13.0 to soak, then mine again. Expect the 48% empty-codex_get rate to drop (that's the acceptance signal for #92). Expect a cleaner aliasResolved column across codex_run and codex_copy. Expect context.next_session to pick up organic usage now that DEFAULT_LLM_INSTRUCTIONS explicitly nudges for it.

HOMEBREW + CI: release workflow triggered on tag push (commands.release completed successfully 2026-04-23). Homebrew tap auto-updates via the existing release job — check seabearDEV/homebrew-ccli after CI completes.
